evidence.metric_deltas: no-edit signature n=33 with dropped_output_rate=0.636 and made_edit_rate=0.061: the model spends output (mean_output_tokens=1146) across mean_tool_call_rounds=2.55 but the turn stops before a patch lands. This is the dropped-output / thinking-stop defect — the rollout emits reasoning/text and the tool-call turn terminates without an edit ever reaching the file.

recommendations.rationale: dropped_output_rate=0.636 with made_edit_rate=0.061 is the signature of opencode's documented behaviour where a turn that produces text-but-no-tool-call simply STOPS (no native retry; #1388/#15906/#29142 per harness-engineering-research.md L6). The only seam that can re-prompt / resample a turn that dropped its tool-call is the repair proxy. Item-16 swept the SCHEMA-expressible sampling/loop levers — L1 anti-repetition REJECTED (Δ inside spread), L6 no-think CONDITIONAL/not-adopted (hurts reasoning fixes), L5 doom_loop REJECTED — and none move 0->>0; this no-text-stop RECOVERY is a distinct, genuinely un-tried structural change (the proxy currently only parse-repairs malformed calls, it does not detect-and-recover a dropped terminal tool-call). It needs proxy code, not a config key, so it is flagged needs-implementation. Honest caveat: the dominant SWE wall is capability-bound (item 16/22), so this is a reliability-floor fix that recovers wasted rollouts, not a guaranteed pass-mover on T3/T4.

needs_implementation.why: The lever schema (sampling/opencode_config/env/system_prompt) cannot express 'detect a turn that produced text but no tool-call and re-prompt/resample for the edit'. opencode has no native retry for a dropped tool-call (turn just stops); the repair proxy is the only seam that sees the raw completion and can inject a recovery turn. This is NOT the item-16 L1/L5/L6 sampling/loop levers (all already A/B'd at K>=3 and rejected); it is a new proxy recovery path.

evidence.metric_deltas: The no-edit signature reports common_errored_tools={edit:4} and the timeout signature {edit:7}: the edit tool errors (old_string not found / gutter or whitespace mismatch) and no patch lands. On 15345/13043 the model locates the site and attempts an edit, but the exact-match edit applier rejects it — an edit-mismatch, distinct from never attempting (no-edit).

recommendations.rationale: edit errors (edit:4 in no-edit, edit:7 in timeout common_errored_tools) on instances where a fix WAS attempted point to gutter/whitespace exact-match rejection. Item-16 L3b already SHIPPED a forgiving matcher in .opencode/tools/edit.ts as correct *insurance*, but the verdict recorded the target defects as INTERMITTENT and L3b was insurance not a pass-mover — so the whitespace-tolerant matcher is only partially exercised, not closed at scale, and remains the genuinely structural (non-schema) seam to harden. It cannot be expressed as a config key (no sampling/env/prompt key changes a string-matcher), so it stays needs-implementation. Honest caveat: per item 16 this is insurance against an intermittent mode, not the binding constraint on the 0/8 capability wall.

needs_implementation.why: A whitespace/indentation-normalising or fuzzy old_string matcher is a string-matching code change in the edit shadow tool; the load_config/apply_levers schema has no key for it. Item-16 L3b began this (forgiving matcher) but only as insurance against an intermittent defect — hardening/coverage is the un-tried work.

evidence.metric_deltas: Under the timeout mode (n=16, timed_out_rate=1.0) sympy-19007 shows the churn signature: mean_tool_call_rounds=34.25 and mean_steps=35.12 with mean_steps_to_first_edit=10.57 — the run burns rounds without converging. This is the 364-round non-termination anchor: very high tool-call-round churn that exhausts the deadline rather than a single slow generation.

recommendations.rationale: The 364-round loop on 19007 is real (high mean_tool_call_rounds under timed_out_rate=1.0), but item-16 L5 (opencode doom_loop / MLX_PROXY loop-control) was explicitly REJECTED: opencode's detector fires only on IDENTICAL repeated calls, whereas this stack's non-termination is VARIED churn or one long slow generation — the wrong detector, SWE timeout 7->7 unchanged at K>=3. So the schema-expressible loop knob is already disproven. A detector that triggers on round-COUNT / no-progress (rounds climbing without steps_to_first_edit advancing) rather than call-identity is a new heuristic that needs proxy/harness code, hence needs-implementation. Honest caveat: digest degenerate_loop_rate=0.0 (the auto-classifier buckets these as timeout); this surfaces the loop as the underlying cause on 19007 but it is a reliability fix on a capability-bound, non-movable tier — low priority, reported for recall not as a climb target.

needs_implementation.why: A no-progress / round-count loop guard (distinct from opencode's identical-call doom_loop, which item-16 L5 rejected as the wrong detector for varied churn) cannot be a config key — it is new detection logic in the proxy/harness that watches tool_call_rounds vs steps-to-first-edit. The schema-expressible loop lever was already A/B'd and rejected.
